If you follow up my lastest post, you can see that it’s being long time since I upload my latest post here, and you may know that I am working and learning for the OSCP exam, which is the ultimate certification in my case. One of the attacks that I have learn on that path and exciting me much, so much so that it gives me chills, is buffer overflow attack. I have seeing many attacks, from the aplication level to the low level, which is called also the infustructor level, in my case I have found my self more enjoyable by learning buffer overflow, and after I finish my first attack on that low memory, I finally recognised what I really what to do on the cyber security field, which is to be cyber security researcher, which mean, among to the others, to know how to analysis malware and viruses, how to tack them, and understand what they desing to do.

If you ask my Mentore, Sagi Kedmi, about that, I am sure that he will be say “you need to be the developer yourself, let the other read you garbage logs”. I can say that I undestand that, but after I develop my last two React-Native apps, I was many time frastrated, then the path I have done untill I have brack my first program in low memory level, the path was so much fun and I learn a lot, so I guess that is better for me to go to that field, the malware researcher.

Let’s brack it done, this post agenda are:

  1. Understand the theoretical behind buffer overflow and how it work.
  2. Learn about the tool we should use over linux, the same that cyber researcher should use.
  3. Perfoming several buffer overflow attacks base on linux programs.

#
